TextENGNew: "You can't tell who it is from just a hand, can you...?"
Sonozaki-san gestured toward the protruding wrist, then without hesitation ripped the futon away.
changeReason: Original says Sonozaki 'turned and pointed to the hand protruding'; new says she 'gestured toward the protruding wrist, then without hesitation ripped the futon away,' adding the decisive action of removing the futon which is in the JPN.

TextENGNew: "You're kidding......?!"
Lying under the futon was... the young man who should have been sleeping here just a while ago.
...It was Takumi Kurosawa.
"Aaahh...!! Nooooooo!!"
"Takumi-kun?!@k@vS10/42/410600224. Why is Takumi-kun — how?! Why?! Aaaahhhh!!"
Towada-san rushed to Kurosawa's side and shook his body. ...But there was no further response.
His complexion had shifted from pale to a dark, mottled hue... He was already gone.
changeReason: Original says skin 'turned a dull pale'; JPN says complexion went from 青白い (pale) to どす黒い (dark/blackish), indicating death; new correctly captures this change to 'dark, mottled hue.'

TextENGNew: "Takumi-kun...?@k@vS10/42/410600226. Takumi-kun, Takumi-kun, Takumi-kun...!!"
Sonozaki-san furrowed her brow and murmured...
changeReason: Original says 'briefly raised an eyebrow'; JPN 眉をしかめて means 'furrowed/knitted her brow' and ぽつりと means 'murmured'; new corrects both.

TextENGNew: "...It's no use.@k@vS10/43/410400275. He's been dead for a while now."
Towada-san clung to Kurosawa's motionless body and spilled tears over top of him.
Miyuki-chan quietly stared as the situation unfolded... but eventually, she moved closer to Towada-san and turned her eyes toward the corpse.
"...Could you give me a moment?"
Towada-san paid no heed to Miyuki-chan's words, and continued crying while clutching Kurosawa's hand.
She didn't want to accept the shock of losing her beloved boyfriend... but reality was dealing her a heavy blow.
"Towada-san..."
"...This is really weird."
"...Otobe?"
Otobe was watching all this from far away as he muttered that in a shaky voice.
"Because... Towada-san said he vanished around the shrine gate, right...?@k@vS10/41/410200418. Then... what is he doing here...?!"
"Because someone carried him here, obviously..."
"...But, when?"
"Well... some time when we weren't here?"
"But... isn't that odd?!@k@vS10/41/410200421. We ran to the shrine gate immediately after hearing her scream... and after that, we came straight here!@k@vS10/41/410200422. Right?!"
"So, when could someone carry him here?!"
"It could've happened while we were standing around on the stone steps for all we know, right?"
"That's still strange!@k@vS10/41/410200425. Aren't the stone steps the only way in and out of the shrine grounds?!@k@vS10/41/410200426. The rest of the area is a sheer cliff!"
"Then, what are you saying?@k@vS10/62/410101022. It's fine to have objections, but you need to substantiate them with a proper explanation!"
"...@k@vS10/41/410200428.If I could do that, I wouldn't be struggling over here..."
Otobe sounded frustrated, then fell silent. ...Then Miyuki-chan stood up, gave him a pat on the shoulder, and turned to us as if taking over for him.
"But... it is a little strange, just as Otobe-kun says.@k@vS10/40/410300642. Why would the culprit stash the body in a place like this...?"
"...If the goal was to hide the corpse, wouldn't it have been easier to just toss it deep in the woods...?"
Terrible thoughts began swirling around inside my head.
If the assailant killed Kurosawa outside and then hid the body in the assembly hall closet, it'd be like they were practically asking us to find it.
On top of that, there was a high risk of being spotted while moving the corpse into the assembly hall closet.
But even so... despite the danger of eyewitnesses, the killer took the trouble of hiding the body in the closet...!
...In which case, the killer had a specific goal in mind.
And that goal was... to reveal the corpse to us... To threaten us...!
"Ryuunosuke-san... what's that over there...?!"
"Isn't that... your cell phone?"
Miyuki-chan pointed toward the entrance of the assembly hall.
Then she rushed over to the doorway, picked up my phone, and came running back.
"What was it doing over there...?"
"Aah, I left it there."
"That's not the criminal's handiwork.@k@vS10/62/410101027. I left it over there... Thanks."
But Miyuki-chan's expression was looking increasingly firm. And then she rested her chin on her right fist, deep in thought.
"...What's the matter?"
"You said you left it here... but why?"
"...Huh?@k@vS10/62/410101030. Oh... it just fell out of my pocket, like what happened to Otobe earlier.@k@vS10/62/410101031. And it's not like it can connect to anything, so I didn't notice."
Aah~, so that's all it is?
...But Miyuki-chan didn't say that.
Miyuki-chan's face gradually clouded over as she listened to my explanation.
"But... isn't that odd...?"
"Because Otobe-kun's phone was torn apart...@k@vS10/40/410300652. So, why wasn't your phone destroyed too...?"
"Well... I wonder?@k@vS10/62/410101034. Maybe because it's not able to connect?"
"...But that's still strange.@k@vS10/40/410300654. Otobe-kun's phone wasn't even usable thanks to the dead battery."

"Kurosawa-san's murderer came through this assembly hall to transport the corpse, right?@k@vS10/40/410300656. If the same culprit was responsible for both these incidents, why would they destroy Otobe-kun's phone but leave Ryuunosuke-san's phone alone...?"
"Well..."
A terrible thought came to mind, and I started feeling nauseous.
The direction this conversation was headed was quite simple... and rather cruel.

"My phone is simply unable to connect...@k@vS10/62/410101037. But aside from the battery issue, there's still a chance that Otobe's phone can connect...@k@vS10/62/410101038. Shouldn't that be reason enough to treat the two phones differently...?"
"If that's the case...@k@vS10/40/410300658. how did the culprit know all that...?"
"Indeed...@k@vS10/62/410101040. You'd think they would have no way of knowing which phones can and can't connect...?"
"Even if the phone didn't get any signal where it was found... there was still a chance it could connect after moving it to another location..."
"Which means..."
"...This is the work of the curse?"
Towada-san said that while still holding Kurosawa's body close to her chest.
"...The curse?"
"Am I wrong?@k@vS10/42/410600235. If this was done by the curse... wouldn't Oyashiro-sama be able to tell which phone is able to connect...?"
Towada-san said that while gazing into the face of her dead lover like he was simply asleep. However, I couldn't agree with her assessment.
"...No, I doubt it.@k@vS10/62/410101044. At least, I think there's a much more likely possibility."
It seemed strange for an occult specialist to suggest this... but I was hesitant to accept the "curse" explanation so easily.
"A-another possibility...?"

"Right, there's a very simple explanation.@k@vS10/62/410101046. All of us knew that particular piece of information.@k@vS10/62/410101047. Which means... one of us is responsible for breaking Otobe's phone."
"...Right.@k@vS10/40/410300661. It's natural to think so."
"B-but... that's not the only explanation, right?!@k@vS10/41/410200432. We were all in the ritual storehouse when Kurosawa-san and Towada-san were attacked!"
"If that's the case, the person who attacked Kurosawa-san and Towada-san and broke my phone can't be one of us..."
"Hey... do you understand what you saying?!"
"I-I understand...!@k@vS10/41/410200435. That's why it has to be someone else who broke my phone and attacked Kurosawa-san and Towada-san...!"
"...Otobe-kun, calm down."
"B-but...!@k@vS10/41/410200437. It's not like Towada-san killed Kurosawa-san, so you're turning your suspicion toward me, aren't you?!"
"Seriously... I'm not the culprit!"
"Nobody's said anything like that. ...Right?"
"...So, can you calm down?"
Otobe was breathing erratically, so Miyuki-chan tried to comfort him. ...But her eyes were still carefully watching his expression.
"Ryuunosuke-san doesn't suspect you.@k@vS10/40/410300666. If that were the case, wouldn't he have just as much reason to suspect me?@k@vS10/40/410300667. ...Right, Ryuunosuke-san?"
"...R-right."
I probably should've said, "It's not like that," right there.
But... I lacked the confidence to actually say it for sure like Miyuki-chan did.
"At any rate... we need to organize our thoughts on everything that's happening!"

"...Not only was the phone broken to prevent us from getting in touch with the outside, but around the same time our cars disappeared to remove our means of escape.@k@vS10/62/410101052. And then... Kurosawa was murdered."
"Putting all that together, I can think of only one fate this is leading us to...!@k@|@y@vS10/62/410101054. ...A massacre!"
"I don't know if the murderer is a human or a ghost of Hinamizawa. But regardless, the "culprit" is keeping us here and planning to pick us off one by one in this bloodbath!"
"So... the first one was taken out as a show of force.@k@vS10/62/410101057. ...Maybe the culprit is even nearby right now, watching us and smirking quietly."
"P... please stop making such distasteful jokes...!"
"He's right, Ryuunosuke-san...@k@vS10/40/410300669. You're going too far..."
"Then, what on earth is this all about?!"
"...It's likely as Arakawa-san says."
"All who step foot in this village are possessed and then killed by the murderous spirits of the dead.@k@vS10/42/410600238. ...First was Takumi-kun, and next is..."
"S... stop it! Not you too, Towada-san...!"
"But... can you think of another explanation?@k@vS10/42/410600240. If that's not the case... then who are you saying killed Takumi-kun?"
Towada-san rubbed Kurosawa's cheek with a hollow look in her eyes as she said that.
None of us... could respond to her declaration.
A vague feeling of anxiety was building up inside of me thanks to that one specific line of reasoning... and I wanted to speak up, to say that idea sounded delusional.
However... I didn't have enough solid evidence... so I couldn't dispute it.
And there was also the fact that I still couldn't dispel my fear and insecurity about this so-called "curse."
"Hehehehe..."
When I turned around, I found Sonozaki-san chuckling to herself while listening to our discussion.
"...Sonozaki-san?"
"Hehehe...@k@vS10/43/410400278. Something so ridiculous can't possibly exist."
"Ridiculous...?@k@vS10/62/410101061. What is that supposed to mean?"
When Sonozaki-san saw the look of protest in my eyes, she let out a heavy sigh.
"It sounds like... you've all been reading too many weird mystery and occult novels."
...That may be true, and even I think it's ridiculous. But I stand my ground.
"Sonozaki-san...@k@vS10/62/410101063. just so you know, I find you more suspicious than any of us."
"Me? Hooh...?"

"I have no idea why you're wandering around in a place like this wearing a kimono.@k@vS10/62/410101065. And walking around carrying a katana is a clear violation of the Swords and Firearms Control Act."
"And more importantly... the person named Mion Sonozaki...@k@vS10/62/410101067. died 20 years ago!"
I hit Sonozaki-san with my trump card.
But her expression didn't change at all.
"So...?@k@vS10/43/410400283. Is there something you want to say?"
"Just... who the hell... are you...?"
"Who am I...? Didn't I tell you earlier?@k@vS10/43/410400285. I'm Mion Sonozaki.@k@vS10/43/410400286. A former resident of Hinamizawa."
"Stop messing around!@k@vS10/62/410101070. What would a dead person be doing wandering around in a place like...@k@vS10/62/410101071. ?!"
Suddenly, the sound of something slicing through the air struck my ear.
When I looked down... I saw Sonozaki-san's sword right against my neck.
"I wouldn't say anything more if I were you.@k@vS10/43/410400288. ...I could slice all of you down in a single blow."
"...It would only take a moment."
After staring at the look of horror on my face for a while, Sonozaki-san returned the sword to its sheath.
"Only the weak would conceal a murder.@k@vS10/43/410400291. Someone like that would be no match for me.@k@vS10/43/410400292. ...So, this has nothing to do with me."
After saying that, Sonozaki-san once again pulled out her scroll and spread it out. She seemed to lose all interest in our conversation and focused her attention exclusively on the scroll.
"Sonozaki-san..."
Otobe tugged at my sleeve as if to say, "Please wait," and then entered the conversation.
"It's just as Arakawa-san says. There are a lot of reasons to be suspicious of Mion-san...@k@vS10/41/410200449. When I first met her, I thought I couldn't trust her..."
"When you... first met...?"
"I first met Mion-san close to here."
"I... got lost.@k@vS10/41/410200452. No matter where I wandered, I couldn't find my way.@k@vS10/41/410200453. On top of that, any time I stopped walking I felt like I could hear an extra footstep..."
"You heard... an extra footstep...?"
"Yeah..."
...I've heard that myself, several times.
Near the suicide car... and within the shrine grounds...
"Hehehe..."
Looking back at the source of that laughter, Sonozaki-san was still staring at the scroll.
What was Sonozaki-san laughing about? Or did I just imagine her laughing? ...I wasn't sure.
"Arakawa-san...?"
"R-right.@k@vS10/62/410101079. It's nothing... What happened next?"
"Next...? Oh, the conversation trailed off a bit. Anyway..."
"So, on top of being separated from everyone, I was hearing those footsteps. I was completely terrified...@k@vS10/41/410200458. I felt totally helpless and didn't know what to do."
"But then I noticed Mion-san standing nearby, and she was nice enough to guide me here...@k@vS10/41/410200460. That's why I'm convinced Mion-san isn't a bad person!"
It sounded like Otobe was trying to appeal specifically to me.
But it was Miyuki-chan who calmly disputed his assertion.
"...You can't judge people simply by their appearance."
"...Miyuki-chan?"
"Indeed, Sonozaki-san may not be a bad person, but...@k@vS10/40/410300672. not all crimes are committed with malicious or selfish intentions..."
"I think there are situations where even a good person needs to kill someone."
Hearing where this conversation was going left a bad taste in my mouth.
The way she said that, it sounded like she had killed someone, herself.
"I don't know Sonozaki-san's history.@k@vS10/40/410300675. But... I don't know your history either.@k@vS10/40/410300676. ...Otobe-kun... did you really drive here with your friends...?"
"...Yes, that's right."
"I wonder if that's really true...?@k@vS10/40/410300678. Did you actually... have friends to begin with?"
Otobe began trembling for some reason.
...Once again, he started acting that way as soon as we began discussing the friends who came here with him.
"W-why are you asking that all of a sudden?!"
However... rather than saying, "Sorry for suspecting you," Miyuki-chan wouldn't leave it at that.
"I don't believe you're telling the truth.@k@vS10/40/410300680. ...That's all."
"W-what's that supposed to mean?!@k@vS10/41/410200466. Why would you accuse me like that?!"
"Why... you ask?@k@vS10/40/410300682. I guess you could call it... a bit of intuition?"
Miyuki-chan stared at Otobe while he bit at his lip. ...But his eyes reminded me of a small animal being stalked by a hunter.

"Didn't you start out saying you were driving to an occult spot with your friends?@k@vS10/40/410300684. ...Sorry, but I don't think you're the sort of person who would enjoy something like that.@k@vS10/40/410300685. You're the kind of person who would be terrified of that sort of thing. ...Right?"
"Plus, you may say that you're worried about your friends... but unlike Towada-san, you've made little effort to search for your companions.@k@vS10/40/410300687. Why is that? ...Please, tell me."
"W-well...!"
Miyuki-chan relentlessly pursued her psychological attack against Otobe.
And one by one, the arguments she made... were all strangely convincing.
As I listened to their exchange, something felt unfathomable about Miyuki-chan's powers of observation.

"I-I am worried about everyone!@k@vS10/41/410200470. Please, don't suggest otherwise!@k@vS10/41/410200471. Plus... my friends were the ones who decided to drive here!@k@vS10/41/410200472. They told me Hinamizawa was a nice place to visit..."
"Well then... how many people did you come here with...?"
"There were... th-three others."
"Three people... so four, including you.@k@vS10/40/410300690. By the way, were you the only male in the group?"
"N-no... there was one other guy.@k@vS10/41/410200475. And two girls..."

"So, some kind of double date?@k@vS10/40/410300692. ...If that's the case, I suppose one of them was your girlfriend?@k@vS10/40/410300693. Aren't you worried about her? For all you know, she could've been killed by the curse..."
"D-didn't I already tell you I'm worried?!@k@vS10/41/410200477. Besides, we're not together!@k@vS10/41/410200478. W-we're just... friends!"
What is this? The feeling of unease that had been accumulating in my chest for a while now... was gradually shaping into suspicion.
Was it directed at Otobe?
Or maybe...?
"...@k@vS10/40/410300695.Otobe-kun... what is it you're hiding?@k@vS10/40/410300696. At the very least... you're not an ordinary student, are you?"
"I-I'm not hiding anything...!"
"I'm just a college student!@k@vS10/41/410200481. What's so weird about me?!@k@vS10/41/410200482. ...B-besides, what about you?!"
"You don't look anything like a freelance writer!"
"...Is that so?@k@vS10/40/410300699. Then what is it? What's strange about me?@k@vS10/40/410300700. Please tell me. I want to understand, ok...?@k@vS10/40/410300701. Right, Ryuunosuke-san?"
Miyuki-chan looked straight at me as she asked that.
I couldn't say anything in response.
...Miyuki-chan certainly didn't look like a freelance writer to me, either.
No... a writer like that certainly could exist, and Otobe was clearly stooping to retaliatory arguments. He was probably just saying the first thing that came to mind. But still...
I still had no idea who Miyuki-chan really was.
"...So, are you satisfied?@k@vS10/40/410300703. Baseless suspicion won't get us anywhere, you know?"
"...R-right."
Miyuki-chan finally ceased her assault and turned to me with a smile.
It was nice that our conversation got back on track, but... seriously, who the hell is this girl?
"A... anyway, a little mutual distrust is only natural in this situation, right?@k@vS10/41/410200485. If we all stick together, no one should be able to lay a hand on us..."
"Well... you're right about that."
"We'll be fine if we stay here until morning.@k@vS10/41/410200487. There's nothing to be afraid of as long as the five of us stay together...!"
"Don't you mean the six of us...?"
"Even though one of us is already dead."
Towada-san was still clinging to Kurosawa.
Staring at the corpse started to give me a headache.
I've got a bad feeling about this...
We were in the middle of a village where demons and the dead dwelled, wandering and hunting down the living.
Could this corpse... rise up like a zombie and start feasting on human flesh...?
"This is... the work of the curse...@k@vS10/42/410600242. I'm sure it's the curse..."
"The curse...?@k@vS10/62/410101087. What do you mean it's the curse?!"
I was so frustrated that I shouted in a pathetic voice.
When it comes to curses and Hinamizawa, the answer is always the curse of Oyashiro-sama...!
Is Oyashiro-sama's curse going to befall us for entering the ritual storehouse...?
Or maybe the aliens who possesses the people of Hinamizawa have awoken and they're trying to control us...? No, that can't be it!
And first off, isn't obsessing over the word curse like that an admission that you've given up on thinking critically?
"Ryuunosuke-san, please calm down..."
"Sonozaki-san!@k@vS10/62/410101090. You're from this village, right?@k@vS10/62/410101091. If you know something, please tell us!"
Sonozaki-san was still reading the scroll. She closed her eyes and lightly rubbed them with both hands, then slowly opened them.
Everyone carefully watched her every move... in complete silence.
Then she rolled up the scroll and tied it with a string like it had been originally.
"Now that you mention it...@k@vS10/43/410400296. this does remind me... of a tale I've heard about Oyashiro-sama.@k@vS10/43/410400297. When someone is chosen as a sacrifice..."
"They start hearing mysterious footsteps... coming from behind."
"Then maybe... you're Oyashiro-sama's messenger..."
"Not a chance!!"
Sonozaki-san's eyes became viciously sharp. ...I couldn't help but choke on my breath after witnessing a level of "rage" I'd never experienced before.

"Naturally, there are many things that I know.@k@vS10/43/410400301. However... the murder that happened here has nothing to do with me.@k@vS10/43/410400302. ...And of course, it has nothing to do with Oyashiro-sama!@k@vS10/43/410400303. Besides..."
"This shoddy incident can't be the curse of Oyashiro-sama!"
Towada-san's spirit was being pushed to the limit. Her shoulders were shaking. Then, Sonozaki-san got up and slowly stepped beside her.
"...Step aside."
"I said step aside."
"Oh... r-right..."
Towada-san timidly moved away from Kurosawa's corpse while Sonozaki-san looked down on the corpse with cold eyes. Then she turned around and faced each of us, one after another.
"...First, we need to ask.@k@vS10/43/410400308. Why did the culprit bring the corpse here...?"
"W-we were talking about that earlier. To threaten..."
"No, you're not thinking here.@k@vS10/43/410400310. Try thinking a little harder."
Sonozaki-san outright dismissed that theory. Then, she relentlessly fixated her gaze straight ahead... toward Towada-san.
"Just how... are we supposed to know that...?"
"Hmm...@k@vS10/43/410400312. Well, let me try to explain my reasoning."
"What if this corpse wasn't carried here...?@k@vS10/43/410400314. What if it was here the whole time?"
"What is that supposed to mean...?!"
"I mean... exactly what I said.@k@vS10/43/410400316. He wasn't brought here from outside, and of course he wasn't attacked outside."
"Then... Kurosawa was..."
"We only ever saw him sleeping wrapped in a blanket.@k@vS10/43/410400318. In other words... Takumi Kurosawa may very well have been dead before we even arrived here."
The air around us froze as soon as Sonozaki-san presented her theory.
She was right, we never actually saw Kurosawa moving around...
But if was the case... it meant... Kurosawa was dead from the very start. But no... he was murdered...?!
If so, the one person who could've killed him was... No way...?!
"Wh... what is this?@k@vS10/62/410101099. Are you seriously suggesting... that Towada-san murdered Kurosawa?!"
"H... hold on, Mion-san!@k@vS10/41/410200492. ...If Towada-san really killed him, why didn't she run away immediately?!@k@vS10/41/410200493. That sounds really weird!"
"Th-that's true..."
I nodded to confirm Otobe's observation.
She had no reason to lie to us about being "attacked" and then come back here.
"Hmm...@k@vS10/43/410400320. That's certainly true..."
Maybe that counterargument got through to her? Sonozaki-san held her hand to her mouth like she was considering it. ...But after a brief thought, she turned toward Towada-san.
...Her shoulders were drooped and she had a look of terror on her face, like she was being stalked by a predator.
"I don't know why this girl came back here.@k@vS10/43/410400322. Maybe she intended to run away,@k@vS10/43/410400323. but some circumstance forced her to return here..."
"W-what kind of circumstance...?"
"Perhaps the truth is...@k@vS10/43/410400325. she made the mistake of "seeing" someone.@k@vS10/43/410400326. Someone that caused her to blame the curse...?"
Towada-san's face froze over.
...Could it be? ...She got it right on the mark?
"...Someone? Who?"
Sonozaki-san continued staring at Towada-san, without acknowledging my question.
...Her gaze had enough power behind it to stop an opponent in their tracks with just a mere glance.
"Towada.@k@vS10/43/410400328. I know you'd rather not show off your dead lover's body, but...@k@vS10/43/410400329. would it be all right... if we gave it a brief examination?"
"Only for a minute... We need to take Kurosawa's body out of the futons to look at it, all right?"
"I told you already... it's because of the curse, ok...?"
Her voice quivered as she said that, doing her utmost to resist. However, her counterargument lacked the power to stop it.
"Indeed, that may be so.@k@vS10/43/410400332. ...However, there's something I want to verify."

"...If Takumi Kurosawa was attacked outside as you told us, his body should still be soaked from the rain.@k@vS10/43/410400334. ...At the very least, the hem of his pants should be caked with rain and mud."
"...Do you mind... if we verify that?"
Towada-san was in absolutely no position to refuse.
Sonozaki-san reached for the futon covering Kurosawa.
But before she could do anything, Towada-san grabbed the futon and pulled it over in a flash.
"...Wait."
Miyuki-chan examined Kurosawa's feet before slowly turning her eyes toward Towada-san.
Kurosawa's socks and the hems of his pants had not a single trace of mud, and no signs of wetness. They were as clean as freshly washed.
"...@k@vS10/42/410600256.Hehehe, ehehehehe...@k@vS10/42/410600257. Ahahahahah... haah..."
"...T-Towada-san?"
"Oh man...@k@vS10/42/410600259. I didn't realize we had a great detective here...@k@vS10/42/410600260. Just a little longer... and I could've regained my peaceful life..."
Towada-san faintly smiled at us.
I felt something rather chilling from her smile.
There was a self-loathing beneath it, an effort to disguise the regret behind earlier mistakes.
"It's just as Sonozaki-san says.@k@vS10/42/410600262. Before all of you arrived here,@k@vS10/42/410600263. ...I killed Takumi-kun."
Sonozaki-san simply stared at Towada-san, without saying a word.
I had no words to respond to Towada-san's incriminating confession.
"...So, when you told us the two of you were attacked by the shrine gate... that was a lie, too...?"
"I'm sorry...@k@vS10/42/410600265. By the time I went to the shrine gate... Takumi-kun was already dead."
Then she paused her explanation for a moment, in thought...
"I... really did... love Takumi-kun...@k@vS10/42/410600267. And because I loved him..."
"...I had to kill him."
